integration.note: An RxDB replication plugin that syncs an RxDB collection to/from a Solid pod via replicateRxCollection. The pod is the replication REMOTE (master); the plugin supplies the pull + push handlers reading/writing one pod resource per document through an INJECTABLE authenticated fetch (the auth seam — never a concrete auth lib). JSON-default storage (each document stored as one application/json resource carrying the WithDeleted master state) with an OPTIONAL injectable toRdf/fromRdf Linked-Data seam (store each document as Turtle/JSON-LD instead, losslessly round-tripping incl. _deleted + the primary key). Checkpoint-based incremental pull over a monotonic per-collection write counter (a total order, wall-clock-independent) held in a small per-collection metadata resource (filtered out of document listings). Tombstone soft-deletes (a _deleted write, not a hard DELETE — so deletions propagate to other clients); hard GC is an explicit SolidDocStore.deleteDoc seam. Conflict resolution is delegated to RxDB's collection conflictHandler — the plugin only DETECTS conflicts (pod master state vs the fork's assumedMasterState) and returns the real master, never picking a winner. Fail-closed scope guard (assertWithinBase on every op) + injective, traversal-proof key sanitisation (keyToResourceName / resourceNameToKey) are the SSRF surface. RDF discipline: the only RDF parsed is the LDP container LISTING (read-only) via @jeswr/fetch-rdf parse + @solid/object ContainerDataset — never hand-built triples. rxdb is a peerDependency (not bundled). GitHub-installable now (committed dist/, ignore-scripts=true); npm publish deferred, not a blocker. Live cross-client pull via WebSocketChannel2023 (call reSync() on a container-change notification) is a DOCUMENTED follow-up seam.
